title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Netanyahu to travel to Washington for Trump meeting, Lindsey Graham funeral
stories.summary:
Netanyahu to travel to Washington for Trump meeting, Lindsey Graham funeral. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu plans to visit Washington on Tuesday for a meeting with President Trump and to attend the funeral service for Sen. Lindsey Graham of South Carolina, a staunch Republican ally of Israel.

Al Jazeera: Netanyahu to meet Trump at White House next week amid Iran war escalation. Al Jazeera reports that visit will be Netanyahu's seventh to White House, underscoring US commitment during war with Iran, genocide in Gaza.

stories.title: Romanian jet fighter shoots down suspected Russian drone
stories.summary:
Romanian jet fighter shoots down suspected Russian drone. The incident adds to concern that Moscow’s war on Ukraine risks spilling over to entangle neighbouring NATO states.

Breitbart: NATO Shoots Down 'Russian' Drone Over Romania For First Time.

Breitbart reports that nATO has shot down what is thought to be a Russian drone over Romanian airspace for the first time after F-16s were deployed to intercept. The post NATO Shoots Down ‘Russian’ Drone Over Romania For First Time appeared first on Breitbart .

stories.title: Inside The Secret Service’s New Security Plan For WHCD
stories.summary:
Inside The Secret Service’s New Security Plan For WHCD. The Secret Service said security will be significantly increased for Friday night’s rescheduled White House Correspondents’ Association Dinner, nearly three months after prosecutors say an armed California man attempted to assassinate President Donald Trump and other administration officials during the annual event.

The dinner, which first took place on April 25 at the Washington Hilton, ... Fox News: Leavitt vows Trump will deliver 'entertaining' speech at his return to White House Correspondents' Dinner.

Fox News reports that karoline Leavitt says President Trump's rescheduled White House Correspondents' Dinner speech will be "unifying yet vicious" and "entertaining.".

stories.title: A global threat for millennia: experts warn Australia’s nuclear submarine legacy will be weapons-usable uranium cache
stories.summary:
A global threat for millennia: experts warn Australia’s nuclear submarine legacy will be weapons-usable uranium cache.

Exclusive: Uranium waste from Aukus submarines will be enriched to an estimated 80%: the same level as the bomb dropped on Hiroshima Australia’s proposed nuclear submarine fleet will leave behind tonnes of weapons-usable uranium-235: radioactive for hundreds of millions of years, and capable of being processed into scores of warheads, nuclear experts have told a public inquiry into Aukus.

And a former White House national security adviser has told the Guardian that Australia’s Auk.

stories.title: OpenAI’s own model went rogue before Kimi had Wall Street sweating
stories.summary:
OpenAI’s own model went rogue before Kimi had Wall Street sweating. Chinese AI lab Moonshot’s open model Kimi went viral this week for reasons that had less to do with the model itself and more to do with how the U.S. AI industry reacted to it.

Meanwhile, an unreleased OpenAI model wandered outside its test environment and ended up connected to a real security breach at Hugging Face — a reminder […].

stories.title: Trump says nearly 200 firms have signed pledge to protect Americans from costs arising from datacenters
stories.summary:
Trump says nearly 200 firms have signed pledge to protect Americans from costs arising from datacenters.

‘Ratepayer Protection Pledge’ president has touted is non-binding as people continue to struggle with rising bills Donald Trump has announced that about 200 entities have signed on to his non-binding “Ratepayer Protection Pledge”, expanding a voluntary commitment which claims to ensure US consumers will not bear the cost of the AI datacenter build-out.

Trump delivered remarks on Thursday at the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) headquarters, alongside Lee Zeldin, the agency’s.

stories.title: EU fines Google €890m for competition breaches over search and apps
stories.summary:
EU fines Google €890m for competition breaches over search and apps. Firm told to treat third-party services that appear in its results in ‘fair and non-discriminatory manner’ Google has been fined a total of €890m (£760m) by the EU for breaches of online competition laws by its search and app store services.

The European Commission, the EU’s executive arm, said Google had broken the Digital Markets Act by giving priority to its own services, such as shopping and hotel deals, in search results over those of its rivals. <a href="https://www.theg.

stories.title: Motorists warned of landslides, flooding on Iloilo-Antique roads
stories.summary:
Motorists warned of landslides, flooding on Iloilo-Antique roads. ILOILO CITY —Authorities on Friday urged motorists to exercise extreme caution when traveling between Iloilo and Antique as heavy rains triggered landslide threats, flooding and road damage along major routes connecting the two provinces.

The Antique Provincial Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (PDRRMC) advised commuters and motorists to avoid the Iloilo-Antique Road via Bukid. Philippine Daily Inquirer: Heavy rains trigger flooding, road damage across Bohol.

Philippine Daily Inquirer reports that tAGBILARAN CITY, Bohol— Heavy rains that swept across Bohol province on Friday triggered flooding, a landslide, road damage, and the collapse of a perimeter fence in several towns, prompting authorities to issue travel advisories and safety warnings.

The persistent rains also triggered a landslide along a road in

links.headline: Heavy rains trigger flooding, road damage across Bohol
links.excerpt: TAGBILARAN CITY, Bohol— Heavy rains that swept across Bohol province on Friday triggered flooding, a landslide, road damage, and the collapse of a perimeter fence in several towns, prompting authorities to issue travel advisories and safety warnings. The persistent rains also triggered a landslide along a road in Barangay Del Pilar, Pilar town. READ: PNP
links.imageAlt: Heavy rains trigger flooding, road damage across Bohol

stories.title: DepEd earns clean audit opinion under Angara reform initiative
stories.summary:
DepEd earns clean audit opinion under Angara reform initiative.

MANILA, Philippines — The Department of Education (DepEd) has significantly improved its compliance with Commission on Audit (COA) recommendations, resolving 83% of its outstanding audit findings in 2025, up from 52% the previous year, as governance reforms under Education Secretary Sonny Angara continue to take effect.

According to COA’s consolidated annual audit report for the year.

stories.title: Legarda hails signing of GIDA Schools Act into law
stories.summary:

MANILA, Philippines – For generations of Filipino families living in the country’s most isolated communities, going to school has often meant walking for hours through mountains, crossing rivers without bridges, riding makeshift boats, or studying in classrooms without electricity and source of water.

stories.title: US accuses American of allegedly wiping his phone using a ‘duress’ password during border search
stories.summary: US accuses American of allegedly wiping his phone using a ‘duress’ password during border search. A U.S. citizen has asked a court to throw out the government's claim that he gave over a passcode to border authorities that wiped his phone's data, opening up fresh questions about a person's constitutional rights at the U.S. border.
